content.content:
# Documentation on Logging, Debugging, and Diagnostics

Based on the Claude Code documentation map provided, here are the relevant pages for your query:

## Direct Matches

**[troubleshooting](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/troubleshooting.md)** covers diagnostic issues including:
- Installation problems on Windows, Linux, and Mac
- "Repeated permission prompts" and authentication challenges
- Performance monitoring ("High CPU or memory usage")
- IDE integration problems
- Markdown formatting issues

**[hooks](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.md)** includes a "Debugging" section with:
- "Basic Troubleshooting" and "Advanced Debugging" approaches
- Debug output examples for hook execution

**[plugins-reference](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins-reference.md)** provides:
- "Debugging and development tools" section
- Debugging commands and common issues

## Related Resources

The **[cli-reference](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/cli-reference.md)** and **[interactive-mode](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/interactive-mode.md)** pages may contain CLI flags or commands relevant to verbose output or diagnostics.

The **[settings](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings.md)** page covers "Environment variables" which often control logging behavior.

Unfortunately, the documentation map doesn't explicitly list dedicated pages for verbose logging modes or comprehensive environment variable diagnostics, though these may exist within the referenced pages.

Web search results for query: "Claude Code debug logging verbose environment variables CLAUDE_DEBUG"

Links: [{"title":"What Is The Verbose Flag In Claude CLI? » Empathy First Media","url":"https://empathyfirstmedia.com/verbose-flag-claude-cli/"},{"title":"DEBUG mode auto-enabled from .env (regression of #346) · Issue #11015 · anthropics/claude-code","url":"https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/11015"},{"title":"Debugging - Model Context Protocol","url":"https://modelcontextprotocol.io/legacy/tools/debugging"},{"title":"Environment Variables | steipete/claude-code-mcp | DeepWiki","url":"https://deepwiki.com/steipete/claude-code-mcp/6.2-environment-variables"},{"title":"ccusage | Claude Code Usage Analysis","url":"https://ccusage.com/guide/environment-variables"},{"title":"how-to-use-claude-code-for-debugging | ClaudeLog","url":"https://claudelog.com/faqs/how-to-use-claude-code-for-debugging/"},{"title":"CLI Debugging Workflow | Cursor & Claude Code | Developer Toolkit","url":"https://developertoolkit.ai/en/claude-code/lessons/debugging/"},{"title":"CLI reference - Claude Code Docs","url":"https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/cli-reference"},{"title":"Claude Code: Best practices for agentic coding","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/claude-code-best-practices"},{"title":"How do I troubleshoot issues with Claude Code output?","url":"https://milvus.io/ai-quick-reference/how-do-i-troubleshoot-issues-with-claude-code-output"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Claude Code debug logging, verbose mode, and environment variables related to CLAUDE_DEBUG:

## Debug Logging Options

### Command-Line Flags
Claude Code provides built-in debugging tools including the `claude doctor` command that runs diagnostics on your installation, the `--verbose` flag for detailed logging during operations, and the `--mcp-debug` flag specifically for Model Context Protocol configuration issues.(no content)You can enable Claude's debug mode with `claude --debug` or set the environment variable `export ANTHROPIC_LOG=debug`.(no content)For debugging Claude invocations, it can be helpful to use the `--verbose` flag. It's generally recommended to turn verbose mode off in production for cleaner output.

### Environment Variables

Claude Code v2.0.32 automatically enables debug mode when it finds `DEBUG=True` in a project's .env file. This appears to be a regression of issue #346, which was marked as fixed in v0.2.54.(no content)There's a suggestion to use a Claude-specific environment variable like `CLAUDE_DEBUG` or `CLAUDE_CODE_DEBUG` instead of the generic `DEBUG` variable to avoid conflicts with application-level debug flags.(no content)As a workaround, you can currently use `DEBUG=False claude` to override the .env setting.

### MCP Debug Logging

The Claude Code MCP Server uses environment variables to customize its runtime behavior without requiring code changes or configuration file modifications. These variables control CLI discovery, debug logging, and execution parameters.(no content)The `MCP_CLAUDE_DEBUG` environment variable controls debug logging. All debug output is written to stderr to avoid interfering with MCP JSON communication on stdout.(no content)When working with MCP, it can be helpful to launch Claude with the `--mcp-debug` flag to help identify configuration issues.

### Debug Log Location

When debug mode is enabled, Claude outputs: "Debug mode enabled Logging to: ~/.claude/debug/<session-id>.txt"


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.content:
# Debugging and Logging in Claude Code

Based on the troubleshooting documentation provided, here's what's available for debugging:

## Diagnostic Commands

The documentation mentions the `/doctor` command for installation health checks: "claude doctor # Check installation health"

This utility helps verify your Claude Code setup is working correctly.

## Troubleshooting Approach

For debugging issues, the guide recommends:

1. **Use `/bug`** - "Use the `/bug` command within Claude Code to report problems directly to Anthropic"

2. **Environment inspection** - Check your setup with commands like `which npm` and `which node` to identify configuration problems

3. **Log examination** - Review configuration files at specific locations:
   - `~/.claude/settings.json` (user settings)
   - `~/.claude.json` (global state)
   - Project-specific files in `.claude/` directories

## Additional Resources

The documentation references environment variables and settings configuration but doesn't provide extensive verbose logging details in the troubleshooting section. For comprehensive debugging information, the guide suggests checking the main settings and MCP documentation sections.

For unresolved issues, you can report problems via GitHub or ask Claude directly about capabilities and features.

content.content:
# Claude Code Debugging and Logging Flags

Based on the CLI reference, here are the key flags for debugging, verbose output, and logging:

## Debug Flag
The `--debug` flag "Enable[s] debug mode with optional category filtering (for example, `"api,hooks"` or `"!statsig,!file"`)". This allows you to selectively enable or exclude specific debug categories to focus on particular aspects of the application.

Example usage:
```
claude --debug "api,mcp"
```

## Verbose Mode
The `--verbose` flag provides comprehensive output throughout your session, described as enabling "verbose logging, shows full turn-by-turn output (helpful for debugging in both print and interactive modes)". This is useful when you need to understand exactly what Claude is processing at each step.

## Output Format Options
For programmatic debugging and monitoring, the `--output-format` flag offers multiple options (`text`, `json`, `stream-json`). The documentation notes that "The `--output-format json` flag is particularly useful for scripting and automation, allowing you to parse Claude's responses programmatically."

These tools work in both interactive and print modes, making them flexible for different debugging scenarios.

# Claude Code Debugging and Logging Flags

Based on the CLI reference, here are the key flags for debugging, verbose output, and logging:

## Debug Flag
The `--debug` flag "Enable[s] debug mode with optional category filtering (for example, `"api,hooks"` or `"!statsig,!file"`)". This allows you to selectively enable or exclude specific debug categories to focus on particular aspects of the application.

Example usage:
```
claude --debug "api,mcp"
```

## Verbose Mode
The `--verbose` flag provides comprehensive output throughout your session, described as enabling "verbose logging, shows full turn-by-turn output (helpful for debugging in both print and interactive modes)". This is useful when you need to understand exactly what Claude is processing at each step.

## Output Format Options
For programmatic debugging and monitoring, the `--output-format` flag offers multiple options (`text`, `json`, `stream-json`). The documentation notes that "The `--output-format json` flag is particularly useful for scripting and automation, allowing you to parse Claude's responses programmatically."

These tools work in both interactive and print modes, making them flexible for different debugging scenarios.

content.content:
# Claude Code: Environment Variables and Logging

## Environment Variables Overview

Claude Code supports extensive environment variable configuration for controlling behavior, authentication, and performance. These can be set directly in your shell or configured in `settings.json` for team-wide rollout.

### Key Environment Variables

**Authentication & API Configuration:**
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` - API credentials for Claude SDK
- `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` - Custom authorization header value
- `ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS` - Additional request headers

**Model Selection:**
- `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` - Override default model choice
- `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` - Model-specific overrides

**Performance & Behavior:**
- `BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS` - Sets shell command timeout duration
- `BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH` - Limits bash output before truncation
- `MAX_THINKING_TOKENS` - Enables extended thinking with token budget
- `CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS` - Maximum output tokens for requests

**Sandbox & Security:**
- `CLAUDE_CODE_SHELL_PREFIX` - Wraps bash commands for logging/auditing
- `DISABLE_PROMPT_CACHING` - Toggle caching across all models

**Optional Features:**
- `DISABLE_TELEMETRY` - Opt out of analytics tracking
- `DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING` - Disable Sentry error collection
- `DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER` - Prevent automatic updates

### Configuration Priority

Settings follow this hierarchy: Enterprise policies → Command-line args → Local project settings → Shared project settings → User settings. Higher-level configurations override lower ones, ensuring enterprise security policies remain enforced.

## Environment Variables Overview

Claude Code supports extensive environment variable configuration for controlling behavior, authentication, and performance. These can be set directly in your shell or configured in `settings.json` for team-wide rollout.

### Key Environment Variables

**Authentication & API Configuration:**
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` - API credentials for Claude SDK
- `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` - Custom authorization header value
- `ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS` - Additional request headers

**Model Selection:**
- `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` - Override default model choice
- `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` - Model-specific overrides

**Performance & Behavior:**
- `BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS` - Sets shell command timeout duration
- `BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH` - Limits bash output before truncation
- `MAX_THINKING_TOKENS` - Enables extended thinking with token budget
- `CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS` - Maximum output tokens for requests

**Sandbox & Security:**
- `CLAUDE_CODE_SHELL_PREFIX` - Wraps bash commands for logging/auditing
- `DISABLE_PROMPT_CACHING` - Toggle caching across all models

**Optional Features:**
- `DISABLE_TELEMETRY` - Opt out of analytics tracking
- `DISABLE_ERROR_REPORTING` - Disable Sentry error collection
- `DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER` - Prevent automatic updates

### Configuration Priority

Settings follow this hierarchy: Enterprise policies → Command-line args → Local project settings → Shared project settings → User settings. Higher-level configurations override lower ones, ensuring enterprise security policies remain enforced.

content.content:
# Debugging Claude Code Hooks

To troubleshoot hooks, Claude Code provides several built-in debugging approaches:

## Basic Troubleshooting Steps

Start with these fundamental checks:

1. **Verify hook registration** - Run `/hooks` command to confirm your hook configuration is loaded
2. **Test your commands independently** - Execute hook scripts manually outside Claude Code first
3. **Ensure script executability** - Confirm scripts have proper permissions
4. **Validate JSON syntax** - Check that your settings files contain valid JSON
5. **Review detailed logs** - Launch with `claude --debug` flag for comprehensive execution traces

## Common Configuration Issues

Watch out for these frequent problems:

- **Unescaped quotes** - Remember to use `\"` for quotes within JSON strings
- **Case-sensitive matchers** - Tool name matching is case-sensitive; verify exact names
- **Path resolution** - Always use absolute paths or `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` for script references

## Advanced Debugging Techniques

For complex issues, employ these strategies:

- **Enable debug mode** - Running `claude --debug` reveals detailed hook execution information
- **Inspect hook input/output** - Validate JSON schemas against actual hook data
- **Monitor environment variables** - Confirm Claude Code's environment is properly configured
- **Test unusual scenarios** - Try edge cases like uncommon file paths
- **Check resource usage** - Verify hooks aren't exhausting system resources
- **Add structured logging** - Implement comprehensive logging within your hook scripts

The debug output shows which hooks execute, what commands run, and their success status with relevant output messages.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile this for the user:

## Claude Code Verbose/Debug Logging - Complete Guide

Based on the official Claude Code documentation, here are all the ways to enable verbose and debug logging:

### 1. **CLI Flags (Recommended Approach)**

The most direct way to see detailed logs is using command-line flags:

#### `--debug` flag with category filtering
```bash
claude --debug
```

Or filter specific categories:
```bash
claude --debug "api,hooks"
claude --debug "api,mcp"
```

You can also exclude categories:
```bash
claude --debug "!statsig,!file"
```

This enables debug mode with detailed execution traces showing which hooks execute, what commands run, and their success status.

#### `--verbose` flag
```bash
claude --verbose
```

This enables "verbose logging, shows full turn-by-turn output" - particularly helpful for debugging in both print and interactive modes. This shows exactly what Claude is processing at each step.

### 2. **Interactive Mode Logging**

Within Claude Code's interactive mode:
- Press **`Ctrl+O`** to toggle verbose output (shows detailed tool usage and execution)
- Press **`?`** to display available shortcuts for your environment

**Note on watch mode:** According to your note that Ctrl+O doesn't work with 'watch', you may need to use the `--debug` or `--verbose` flags as command-line arguments instead, since they apply to the entire session and work independently of watch mode.

### 3. **Environment Variables**

Set these for persistent debug behavior across sessions:

#### For general debugging
```bash
export ANTHROPIC_LOG=debug
```

#### For MCP-specific debugging
```bash
export MCP_CLAUDE_DEBUG=1
```

All MCP debug output is written to stderr to avoid interfering with JSON communication on stdout.

**Important note:** Claude Code v2.0.32+ automatically enables debug mode when it finds `DEBUG=True` in a project's `.env` file. If you don't want this, use:
```bash
DEBUG=False claude
```

### 4. **Log File Locations**

When debug mode is enabled, Claude outputs:
```
Debug mode enabled
Logging to: ~/.claude/debug/<session-id>.txt
```

You can inspect these files for complete execution history.

### 5. **Diagnostic Command**

For installation and setup issues:
```bash
claude doctor
```

This checks your installation health and can help identify configuration problems.

### 6. **Configuration Files to Inspect**

Review these when debugging:
- `~/.claude/settings.json` - user settings
- `~/.claude.json` - global state
- `.claude/` directories in your project - project-specific settings

### 7. **Recommended Approach for Watch Mode**

Since you mentioned Ctrl+O doesn't work with 'watch', I recommend:

1. **For continuous monitoring:** Use the flag when starting Claude:
   ```bash
   claude --debug "api,hooks" --verbose
   ```

2. **For hook debugging specifically:** Run with debug enabled to see detailed hook execution information including which hooks execute, their input/output, and success status.

3. **For MCP debugging:** Use the `--mcp-debug` flag to identify configuration issues:
   ```bash
   claude --mcp-debug
   ```

### 8. **For Hooks-Specific Debugging**

Based on your custom slash commands, when debugging hooks:
1. Run `/hooks` command to confirm your hook configuration is loaded
2. Launch with `claude --debug` flag for comprehensive execution traces
3. Ensure you're using absolute paths or `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` in your hook scripts

Sources:
- [Claude Code CLI Reference - Debug and Verbose Flags](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/cli-reference.md)
- [Claude Code Troubleshooting Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/troubleshooting.md)
- [Claude Code Settings and Environment Variables](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/settings.md)
- [Claude Code Hooks Debugging](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.md)
- [Claude Code Interactive Mode Documentation](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/interactive-mode.md)
- [What Is The Verbose Flag In Claude CLI](https://empathyfirstmedia.com/verbose-flag-claude-cli/)
- [DEBUG mode auto-enabled from .env (regression) - GitHub Issue](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/11015)
